title: Revert "Support Windows11 As A sshuttle Server"
body:
Reverts sshuttle/sshuttle#993

This change was made in error. See discussion in #993.

step_plan.summary: Revert the mistaken addition of Windows (Win32) server support by preventing server startup on Windows, disabling Windows-specific firewall daemon setup, and auditing method auto-selection and windivert support so Windows-only methods are not inadvertently used for server-mode.

steps.operation: Add early platform guard to refuse server startup on Windows
steps.reason: Prevent the SSHuttle server process from running on Windows (sys.platform == 'win32') so Windows-specific server code paths (SocketRWShim, Windows route parsing, hostwatch alternatives) are never exercised. This is the minimal, explicit revert: fail fast and produce a clear message when someone attempts to run the server on Windows.
steps.side_effects: All attempts to launch the server on Windows will raise a Fatal (or exit) with a clear message. Client-only Windows use (e.g., running sshuttle on Windows as a client) remains unaffected. Tests that previously expected server startup on Windows must be updated/removed. Any tooling or docs that advertised Windows server support must be reverted.

steps.operation: Make start_hostwatch explicitly POSIX-only (fail on Windows)
steps.reason: start_hostwatch uses os.fork() and other POSIX-only behavior. Although server.main will now refuse to run on Windows, add a defensive check in start_hostwatch so that calling it on win32 fails predictably instead of failing in an OS-specific way later.
steps.side_effects: Calling start_hostwatch on Windows will raise a clear Fatal error. This is defensive: it doesn't change client behavior, but any code paths that incorrectly call start_hostwatch on Windows will now fail earlier and more clearly. Unit tests that call start_hostwatch directly on Windows will need adjustment.

steps.operation: Prevent Windows route parsing from being used in server mode
steps.reason: If server-mode is disabled on Windows, the Windows-specific route-detection codepath should not run. Make list_routes no-op / return an empty list (or log a warning) on sys.platform == 'win32' to avoid accidental use of Windows route detection for a server.
steps.side_effects: If any code expects list_routes to return routes on Windows in server contexts, that expectation will break; however, since server startup is blocked on Windows, this is a low-risk change. Tests that assert route discovery on Windows in server contexts must be updated.

steps.operation: Disable or make Windows firewall daemon setup fail with a clear message
steps.reason: Reverting Windows-server support requires disabling the firewall daemon setup that was added for Windows servers. Change _setup_daemon_for_windows to raise Fatal (or otherwise refuse to run) so Windows-specific daemon plumbing is not used.
steps.side_effects: Attempts to start the firewall daemon on Windows will now fail with a clear error. Unix firewall daemon mode remains unchanged. Tests and docs that described running the firewall daemon on Windows must be updated. This reduces the attack surface/maintenance burden by removing a rarely tested Windows server codepath.

steps.operation: Rebind setup_daemon so Windows does not expose a working daemon setup
steps.reason: At module load the code binds setup_daemon to the platform-specific implementation. Update the binding so that on sys.platform == 'win32' setup_daemon either points to a function that raises Fatal or is otherwise disabled. This prevents higher-level code from attempting to start a firewall daemon on Windows even if it tries to call setup_daemon.
steps.side_effects: Any code path that calls firewall.setup_daemon on Windows will now receive a consistent Fatal error. On Unix, setup_daemon behavior is unchanged. Tests that mock or call setup_daemon on Windows must be updated. API shape is preserved (function exists) but behavior on Windows is restricted.

steps.operation: Audit and make get_auto_method explicitly avoid selecting Windows-only server methods
steps.reason: Ensure method auto-selection does not inadvertently choose Windows-only methods (e.g., windivert) for server-mode usage. Add an explicit, backward-compatible guard (for example, an optional server_mode=False parameter) and filter out Windows-only methods when server_mode is True or when running on sys.platform == 'win32' and server behavior would be inappropriate.
steps.side_effects: This is a conservative, backward-compatible change: the added optional parameter has a default so existing callers continue to work. Tests that rely on selecting windivert for server contexts will need to be adjusted. Client-side method selection on Windows remains supported. The change reduces risk of accidentally enabling a Windows server method.

steps.operation: Restore/ensure conservative support check for Windivert method
steps.reason: Ensure the windivert Method advertises support only for Windows clients (sys.platform == 'win32') and does not broaden support to imply Windows server capability. Revert any expansion that would allow windivert to be used in server-side contexts.
steps.side_effects: Windivert will continue to be selectable on Windows clients only. Any previous changes that advertised server-side windivert support will be undone, preventing server-mode selection of this method. Tests that relied on a widened is_supported behavior for server use must be updated; client tests remain valid.
